name: Project.Feature.NodeTree.StartupColorPersistence
observations: Implemented persistent file coloring that survives application restarts by checking existing file content when tree loads.
Integration: _check_file_color_on_startup method called from _create_file_item, uses LogWriter.get_file_line_count to read content.
Color thresholds: 0 lines=red (no content), <10 lines=yellow (minimal), ≥10 lines=green (sufficient content).

name: Project.Method.NodeTree.CheckFileColorOnStartup
observations: Method in NodeTreePresenter that applies colors to file items based on existing content during tree population.
Located in src/commander/presenters/node_tree_presenter.py, called after file_item_map insertion.
Logic: Check file existence → count lines → apply color (red/yellow/green) → log action.

name: Project.Pattern.NodeTree.DualModeColoring
observations: Pattern implementing two coloring modes: startup mode (content-only) and runtime mode (execution-aware).
Startup mode validates existing files without command status, runtime mode uses command_success + log_success + line_count.
Both modes converge on same visual result for files with content, ensuring color consistency across restarts.
